description: Comprehensive performance audit fix: remove redundant auth checks, parallelize sequential queries, defer synchronous side-effects, clean up dead cache invalidation, and fix N+1 query patterns. Total estimated savings: 500ms-3s+ on entity creation flows, 100-400ms on page loads. VERIFIED by 4 Opus agents before implementation.

userStories.title: Add missing conversation_participants index
userStories.description: REVISED after verification: All 7 originally proposed indexes ALREADY EXIST. The only missing index is conversation_participants(thread_id, user_id) — needed for the US-012 RPC unread count function. This composite index supports the most frequent participant lookup pattern (find user's participation in a specific thread).
acceptanceCriteria: Migration file created with single index: idx_conversation_participants_thread_user ON conversation_participants(thread_id, user_id)
No duplicate indexes created (verified against existing migration 20260130230000)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFICATION: All 7 originally proposed indexes already exist in migrations 20251009000001, 20251023100322, 20260130230000. Only conversation_participants(thread_id, user_id) composite is missing.

userStories.title: Remove redundant auth checks in server actions
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: All 5 targets use createServerActionSupabaseClient() (authenticated, RLS-enforced). Zero service role usage in target operations. RLS policies: buildings_update uses is_team_manager(team_id), lots_update uses is_team_manager(get_lot_team_id(id)), contracts use user_belongs_to_team_v2/can_manage_contract, time_slot_responses has INSERT/UPDATE policies. Targets: (1) building-actions.ts ~lines 201-230: remove team_members query, (2) lot-actions.ts ~lines 86-110: remove team_members query, (3) contract-actions.ts: delete verifyTeamAccess() function + all calls, (4) intervention-actions.ts acceptTimeSlotAction: remove 3-query pre-flight, (5) cancelTimeSlotAction: remove team_members role check. Trade-off: lose specific error messages, replaced by generic PostgREST errors.
acceptanceCriteria: verifyTeamAccess function deleted from contract-actions.ts
Manual team_members queries removed from building-actions, lot-actions
Pre-flight permission checks removed from acceptTimeSlotAction, cancelTimeSlotAction
All actions still return proper errors via RLS (not found = no access)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: All 5 use authenticated client. RLS covers all cases. No service role in target ops.

userStories.title: Parallelize intervention edit page queries (2 waves)
userStories.description: VERIFIED PARTIAL: File app/gestionnaire/(no-navbar)/interventions/modifier/[id]/page.tsx. Wave 1 (parallel): buildings, lots, teamMembers, assignments, timeSlots, quotes, documents — all use only team.id or URL param id. Wave 2 (parallel after wave 1): signedUrls (needs documents from wave 1) + tenants (needs intervention.lot_id/building_id from wave 1 gate query). Service instantiations also parallelizable (stateless factories).
acceptanceCriteria: Wave 1: 7+ queries wrapped in Promise.all
Wave 2: signedUrls + tenants in Promise.all (after wave 1)
Service instantiations parallelized in their own Promise.all
Page still renders correctly with all data
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: J(signedUrls) depends on I(documents), K(tenants) depends on B(intervention). All others independent.

userStories.title: Parallelize new entity page queries (recurring occupiedLotIds pattern)
userStories.description: VERIFIED PARTIAL: All 5 pages share the same pattern: raw DB queries are independent (buildings, occupiedLotIds, lots, contacts/types all use only team.id), but TRANSFORMS are sequential (transform buildings needs B+C, transform lots needs C+D+E). Correct approach: Wave 1 = all raw queries in Promise.all, Wave 2 = sequential JS transforms (no DB, just CPU). Pages: (1) contrats/nouveau, (2) contrats/modifier/[id], (3) contacts/nouveau, (4) nouvelle-intervention, (5) immeubles/nouveau + lots/nouveau, (6) contrats/[id] service init.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 page files updated with Promise.all for raw DB queries (Wave 1)
Transforms remain sequential after Promise.all (Wave 2, JS only)
Service instantiations parallelized where sequential
No functional regression (pages render same data)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: occupiedLotIds transforms depend on multiple raw queries. Raw queries are safe to parallelize, transforms must stay sequential.

userStories.title: Parallelize create-manager-intervention API (services + threads)
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: (1) 4 service instantiations are stateless factories — safe to Promise.all. (2) Thread creation: 5 independent chains (group, tenants_group, individual tenants, providers_group, individual providers). No unique constraint conflicts since each has different (intervention_id, thread_type, participant_id) tuple. CRITICAL: all threads MUST complete BEFORE assignment insertion (trigger add_assignment_to_conversation_participants needs threads to exist). (3) Remove redundant user ID validation query.
acceptanceCriteria: Service instantiations use Promise.all (4 parallel)
Thread creation chains parallelized (5 independent chains)
Thread creation completes BEFORE assignment insertion (ordering preserved)
Redundant user validation query removed
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: No unique constraint conflicts. Thread-before-assignment ordering is critical.

userStories.title: Parallelize dashboard queries (prestataire, locataire, admin)
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: (1) Prestataire: interventions + unread threads are independent (different inputs: team.id vs profile.id). (2) Locataire: tenant data + unread threads independent (both use profile.id but different tables). (3) Admin: service inits parallelizable, but stats query depends on user query result (partial). (4) Contact details: invitation check can join Promise.all. (5) Company details: company + contacts parallel (contacts uses URL param, not company result).
acceptanceCriteria: Prestataire dashboard: interventions + unread in Promise.all
Locataire dashboard: tenant data + unread in Promise.all
Admin dashboard: service inits in Promise.all
Contact details: invitation in Promise.all
Company details: company + contacts in Promise.all
Lint passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: All targets confirmed independent. Admin stats depends on user query (keep sequential).

userStories.title: Batch contract rent reminder creation (replace N sequential actions)
userStories.description: REVISED: Cannot defer to after() because redirect target page (/gestionnaire/contrats/[id]) SSR-fetches interventions via interventionService.getByContract(id) and displays them immediately. Instead: create a createBatchRentReminders() server action that does 1 auth check, 1 subscription check, and 1 bulk insert for all 12 reminders instead of 12 × (auth + sub check + insert + revalidation). Also defer createContractNotification() to after() since it runs AFTER all reminders are created.
acceptanceCriteria: New createBatchRentReminders server action in contract-actions.ts or intervention-actions.ts
Single auth check and subscription check for all reminders
Bulk DB insert for all reminder interventions
createContractNotification moved to after() (runs after reminders exist)
Contract creation with 12 reminders completes in <1s instead of 5s+
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: createBatchRentRemindersAction + client updated. createContractNotification made fire-and-forget.

userStories.title: Bulk contract contact insertion (replace for loop)
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: addContractContact does only auth + team check + DB insert + cache invalidation. No notifications, no activity logs per contact. UNIQUE(contract_id, user_id, role) protects duplicates. Order doesn't matter. Create addContractContactsBatch() with 1 auth check, 1 bulk insert, 1 cache invalidation.
acceptanceCriteria: New addContractContactsBatch server action in contract-actions.ts
Single auth check, single bulk DB insert, single cache invalidation
Client calls batch action instead of loop
Existing addContractContact kept for backward compat
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: addContractContactsBatch + client updated. Existing addContractContact kept for edit mode.

userStories.title: Defer contact invitation email to after()
userStories.description: VERIFIED with CAUTION: In /api/invite-user/route.ts, generateLink() CANNOT be deferred (produces hashed_token for user_invitations record). But sendInvitationEmail() and activity_logs insert CAN be deferred. All DB records created before email. Caveat: response will always return invitationSent: true optimistically. after() is confirmed available (Next.js 15.2.6, already used in 17+ files).
acceptanceCriteria: sendInvitationEmail moved to after() callback
activity_logs insert moved to after() callback
generateLink() stays synchronous (required for invitation token)
API response returns after DB operations complete
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: sendInvitationEmail + activity_logs moved to after(). generateLink stays sync.

userStories.title: Remove ALL dead cache invalidation code
userStories.description: VERIFIED: ALL revalidatePath AND revalidateTag calls across ALL server actions are DEAD CODE. Reason: every page is force-dynamic (explicit export or implicit via cookies() call in getServerAuthContext), and no service/repository uses next:{tags} or unstable_cache. Remove all revalidatePath and revalidateTag calls from ALL server action files. This eliminates synchronous no-op work on every mutation.
acceptanceCriteria: All revalidatePath calls removed from server actions in app/actions/
All revalidateTag calls removed from server actions in app/actions/
Helper functions like revalidateInterventionCaches removed or emptied
Pages still load fresh data (they are force-dynamic, no cache involved)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: ~85 dead revalidatePath/revalidateTag calls removed from 7 files. ~120 total lines removed. auth-actions.ts kept (login/signup layout revalidation).

userStories.title: Cache Stripe subscription info in gestionnaire layout
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: getSubscriptionInfo returns SubscriptionInfo (plain object with primitives — fully JSON-serializable, no Stripe objects). Wrap with unstable_cache(15min TTL, team-scoped key). MUST also add revalidateTag('subscription') in Stripe webhook handler for immediate invalidation on subscription changes.
acceptanceCriteria: getSubscriptionInfo wrapped with unstable_cache (15min, tag: 'subscription')
Cache key includes team.id to prevent cross-team pollution
revalidateTag('subscription') added in Stripe webhook handler
Layout uses cached version
Billing page still shows fresh data after subscription change
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: getCachedSubscriptionInfo with unstable_cache(15min, tags:subscription). Webhook revalidateTag added.

userStories.title: Create RPC for thread unread counts (replace N+1)
userStories.description: As a user, I want faster intervention detail page load. Create get_thread_unread_counts(p_thread_ids uuid[], p_user_id uuid) SQL function. Must handle edge cases: (1) NULL last_read_message_id = count ALL messages, (2) deleted last_read message = return 0 (match current behavior), (3) non-participant = count all messages (match current behavior). Function must be SECURITY DEFINER to bypass RLS on conversation tables. Apply to all 3 role pages.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL function get_thread_unread_counts created via migration
Handles NULL last_read_message_id (counts all messages)
Handles deleted last_read message (returns 0)
Handles non-participant (counts all messages)
Gestionnaire intervention detail page uses RPC
Locataire intervention detail page uses RPC
Prestataire intervention detail page uses RPC
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: SQL RPC get_thread_unread_counts + all 3 role pages updated. SECURITY DEFINER.

userStories.title: Stats optimizations + contrats page background jobs
userStories.description: VERIFIED SAFE: (1) head:true on count-only queries in stats.repository.ts — currently fetches all rows just for count, (2) transitionContractStatuses + checkExpiringContracts already fire-and-forget — moving to after() is semantically cleaner with identical behavior, (3) getInterventionStats needs teamId filter (currently no filter = full table scan through RLS).
acceptanceCriteria: All count-only queries use { count: 'exact', head: true }
Contract background jobs wrapped in after() in contrats page
getInterventionStats accepts and uses teamId parameter
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DONE: head:true on all count-only queries. after() for contract status transitions + expiry checks. getInterventionStats has no callers (dead code).
